content.text:
Now I have a clear 3D view of the molecule with all atom labels visible. Let me carefully examine atom C:24 (carbon, index 24) and identify its neighbor atoms by looking at the bonds connected to it.

From the image, I can see atom C:24 is located in the lower central part of the structure. Looking at the bonds directly connected to C:24, I can identify:

1. C:24 is connected to C:23 (carbon, index 23) - to the left
2. C:24 is connected to C:25 (carbon, index 25) - to the right
3. C:24 is connected to C:28 (carbon, index 28) - below
4. C:24 is connected to H:53 (hydrogen, index 53) - above (small white sphere)

Wait, let me check more carefully. Looking at the labels around C:24:
- To the left: C:23 (I can see "C:23" label)
- To the right: C:25 (I can see "C:25" label)
- Below: C:28 (I can see "C:28" label)
- Above: H:53 (I can see "H:53" label)

Yes, these four atoms are directly bonded to C:24. The molecule appears to be a complex organic structure with C:24 being a carbon atom in what looks like a ring or junction point.

The geomconf_index is clearly 2581 from the filename "geomconf_2581.xyz".

So the neighbor atoms of atom 24 (C) are:
- Atom 23: C
- Atom 25: C
- Atom 28: C
- Atom 53: H

And the geomconf_index is 2581.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 24, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
